Motion: This house prefers a world in which, upon childhood, all people intuitively come to know their natural expiration date. Information Slide: For the purposes of this debate, a person’s “natural expiration date” is the date at which they will certainly die from natural causes (e.g., old age, disease, etc.). They can still die earlier by non-natural causes like murder, accidents, etc. Furthermore, one’s natural expiration date is always accurate and cannot be extended.
This house believes that the criminal justice system should not consider retribution as a factor in sentencing.
This house believes that the criminal justice system should not consider retribution as a factor in sentencing.

Motion: When responding to civilian casualties, this house believes that the military action taken does not have to meet the principle of proportionality. Information Slide: The principle of proportionality asserts that an act of war is just if and only if the harm caused by it is proportionate to the harm intended to be inflicted by the opposing warring party.
